title: Oracle Stock Doubles in a Year. Will the Rally in ORCL Last? Despite the strong run-up, Wall Street continues to favor Oracle, maintaining a “Strong Buy” consensus rating.

Are You Listening When the Oracle Speaks? Oracle (ORCL) shows strong technical momentum, hitting new 52-week highs as recently as July 31. ORCL maintains a 100% “Buy” opinion from Barchart indicators. Fundamentals are robust, with double-digit revenue and earnings growth projected for the next two years and a $718 billion market cap. Analyst sentiment is overwhelmingly bullish,...
These 3 Technology Stocks Are Money-Printing Machines All three are exceptional picks for shareholders.

Oracle Helps Customers Achieve Extreme Availability and Performance for Mission-Critical and Agentic AI Applications Oracle today announced the general availability of Oracle Globally Distributed Exadata Database on Exascale Infrastructure, designed to simplify the deployment of distributed mission-critical applications across Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) regions worldwide. This new service automatically distributes, stores, and synchronizes data across multiple locations, enabling applications to remain online even during regional outages and helping businesses address data residency requirements.

Semperis Appoints Randall Belknap to Lead U.S. Federal Sales and Partner Strategy Semperis, a provider of AI-powered identity-security and cyber resilience, today announced the appointment of Randall Belknap as head of U.S. Federal Sales. A seasoned executive with more than 30 years of experience in federal enterprise software sales, Belknap will lead Semperis' public sector go-to-market strategy, drive strategic growth in U.S. federal accounts, and oversee the expansion of the company's federal partner network and channel distribution initiatives.

Celent Recognizes Oracle for Excellence in Digital Banking Platforms Celent has recognized Oracle Financial Services across six of its recently published research reports evaluating global digital banking platforms for corporate, retail, and SMB banking. The recognition underscores Oracle's continued leadership and innovation in delivering scalable, secure, and feature-rich digital banking solutions to financial institutions worldwide.

AI accounting startup Rillet raises $70 million in Andreessen Horowitz, ICONIQ-led round (Reuters) -Rillet, a startup developing AI-powered accounting software, has raised $70 million in a fundraise co-led by venture capital firms Andreessen Horowitz and ICONIQ in a bid to disrupt an industry dominated by tools from Oracle and Microsoft.  A source close to the deal said the startup was valued at around $500 million.  The round was joined by Sequoia, Oak HC/FT and FOG Ventures, the company said on Wednesday, and comes just over two months after Rillet raised $25 million, bringing its total funding to more than $100 million.
